entities.name: TOMATO
entities.description: Solanum lycopersicum L., the agricultural plant being studied for disease tolerance

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A devastating fungal disease that reduces yield and production in tomato

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. LYCOPERSICI
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing Fusarium wilt

entities.name: XSP10
entities.description: Xylem sap protein 10, a putative negative regulatory gene associated with Fusarium wilt susceptibility

entities.name: SLSAMT
entities.description: Salicylic acid methyl transferase, a putative negative regulatory gene associated with Fusarium wilt susceptibility

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of tomato plants to resist or tolerate Fusarium wilt disease

entities.name: CRISPR/CAS9
entities.description: A high-efficiency gene-editing technique used to knock out susceptibility genes

entities.name: RNAI
entities.description: An alternative gene-silencing method used to knock down susceptibility genes

entities.name: INDEL MUTATIONS
entities.description: Insertions and deletions in the DNA sequence resulting from CRISPR/Cas9 editing

entities.name: PROTOPLAST TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: A single-cell transformation technique used to validate sgRNA-Cas9 editing efficacy

entities.name: TRANSIENT LEAF DISC ASSAY
entities.description: An assay used to evaluate phenotypic tolerance to Fusarium wilt in edited tissues

entities.name: STABLE GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of creating stable transgenic lines of tomato
